name: stack
description: Overlaps children in a CSS grid stack with a configurable offset to create a card-pile effect

meta.name: Stack
meta.description: Overlaps children in a CSS grid stack with a configurable offset to create a card-pile effect

props.name: offset
props.description: Pixel offset applied per layer in both axes

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: Card pile
examples.description: Three cards stacked with a 6px offset to show depth

examples.title: Tight stack
examples.description: Overlapping avatar group with minimal offset

whenToUse: Card pile UI where depth and layering should be visible
whenNotToUse: Normal vertical lists — use a flex column or VStack instead
Independent positioned elements — use CSS position directly

related.name: Avatar
related.reason: Overlapping avatar groups are a primary use case for Stack

related.name: Card
related.reason: Card pile visualisations are a common Stack pattern

intent.a11yRationale: Stack is a layout-only container (role="none"). Each child must carry its own accessible semantics. Stacked cards that are interactive should each have a focusable element and a descriptive label.

flexibility.note: The pixel offset is fully configurable; set to 0 for a pure overlap with no shift
